RQ1,RQ1.txt::chunk2,". Architecturally, the EST service is located between a Certification Authority (CA) and a client. It performs several functions traditionally allocated to the Registration Authority (RA) role in a PKI. The nature of communication between an EST server and a CA is not described in this document. EST adopts the Certificate Management Protocol (CMP) [RFC4210] model for CA certificate rollover, but it does not use the CMP message syntax or protocol",NO_TEST_CASE,no_assertion_evidence,weak,True,"The requirement is architectural in nature: EST sits between a CA and a client, performs RA-like functions, the nature of EST<->CA communication is not described, and EST adopts the CMP model for CA certificate rollover but does not use CMP message syntax. Candidate tests are empty and provided unit tests do not assert or observe server-side behavior such as forwarding enroll/reenroll requests to a signing authority or verifying CMP-modeled rollover behavior."
